---
title: "Welcome"
title-block-banner: true
page-layout: article
---

# Welcome {.unnumbered}

A structured, project-based path from Python fundamentals to production-ready
data science and MLOps — software engineering standards applied to the full
data science lifecycle.

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## How this book works

Every chapter is a Jupyter notebook meant to be **run top to bottom**, not just read.
Along the way you will see four recurring markers:

| Marker | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| <i class="bi bi-info-circle-fill" style="color:#0369A1"></i> **Key Concept** | The core idea, precisely defined |
| <i class="bi bi-puzzle-fill" style="color:#EA580C"></i> **Activity** | Hands-on challenge — fill in the `...` yourself before moving on |
| <i class="bi bi-bug-fill" style="color:#DC2626"></i> **Common Mistake** | A bug pattern worth recognising on sight |
| <i class="bi bi-lightbulb-fill" style="color:#6B7280"></i> **Pro Tip** | A production-grade habit |
:::

## What's inside

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div style='border:1px solid #DEE2E6;border-radius:8px;padding:18px 20px;height:100%'>
<span style='background:#EAF7F0;color:#059669;font-weight:bold;font-size:0.75em;padding:3px 10px;border-radius:999px'>COMPLETE</span>

### Part 1 — Python Basics

Language core, control flow, professional patterns, NumPy, and data visualisation with Matplotlib, Lets-Plot, and Seaborn.

- [01 — Python Core](tutorials/01-python-basics/01-python-core.ipynb)
- [02 — Control Flow](tutorials/01-python-basics/02-control-flow.ipynb)
- [03 — Python Patterns](tutorials/01-python-basics/03-python-patterns.ipynb)
- [04 — NumPy](tutorials/01-python-basics/04-numpy.ipynb)
- [05 — Matplotlib and Its Ecosystem](tutorials/01-python-basics/05-matplotlib.ipynb)
- [06 — Grammar of Graphics with Lets-Plot](tutorials/01-python-basics/06-lets-plot.ipynb)
- [07 — Data Storytelling and House Style](tutorials/01-python-basics/07-data-storytelling.ipynb)
</div>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div style='border:1px solid #DEE2E6;border-radius:8px;padding:18px 20px;height:100%'>
<span style='background:#EAF7F0;color:#059669;font-weight:bold;font-size:0.75em;padding:3px 10px;border-radius:999px'>COMPLETE</span>

### Part 2 — Data Analysis

Pandas 3, Polars, time series, and exploratory data analysis on real tabular data.

- [08 — Pandas Core](tutorials/01-python-basics/08-pandas-core.ipynb)
- [09 — Pandas Operations](tutorials/01-python-basics/09-pandas-operations.ipynb)
- [10 — Combining, Reshaping & Time Series](tutorials/01-python-basics/10-combining-reshaping.ipynb)
- [11 — Polars & the Expression API](tutorials/01-python-basics/11-polars.ipynb)
</div>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div style='border:1px solid #DEE2E6;border-radius:8px;padding:18px 20px;height:100%;opacity:0.7'>
<span style='background:#F4F5F6;color:#6B7280;font-weight:bold;font-size:0.75em;padding:3px 10px;border-radius:999px'>COMING SOON</span>

### Part 3 — Dev Tools

Git workflow, `uv`, `ruff`, pre-commit, pytest, and `mypy` — the engineering
practices every chapter from here on assumes.
</div>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div style='border:1px solid #DEE2E6;border-radius:8px;padding:18px 20px;height:100%;opacity:0.7'>
<span style='background:#F4F5F6;color:#6B7280;font-weight:bold;font-size:0.75em;padding:3px 10px;border-radius:999px'>COMING SOON</span>

### Part 4 — Classical ML & Deep Learning

Pipelines, hyperparameter tuning with Optuna, and a first pass at PyTorch.
</div>
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
<div style='border:1px solid #DEE2E6;border-radius:8px;padding:18px 20px;height:100%;opacity:0.7'>
<span style='background:#F4F5F6;color:#6B7280;font-weight:bold;font-size:0.75em;padding:3px 10px;border-radius:999px'>COMING SOON</span>

### Part 5 — MLOps

System design, packaging, CI/CD, and serving a model in production.
</div>
:::

:::

Later parts are added chapter by chapter as each one reaches the same standard as Parts 1 and 2.

## Get started

<a href="tutorials/01-python-basics/01-python-core.ipynb" style="background:#1E293B;color:white;padding:10px 20px;border-radius:6px;text-decoration:none;font-weight:bold;display:inline-block;margin-top:8px"><i class="bi bi-play-fill"></i> Start with Part 1</a>
<a href="tutorials/01-python-basics/08-pandas-core.ipynb" style="background:#0369A1;color:white;padding:10px 20px;border-radius:6px;text-decoration:none;font-weight:bold;display:inline-block;margin-top:8px;margin-left:8px"><i class="bi bi-table"></i> Jump to Part 2</a>

Source code: [github.com/sambaiga/ds-mlops-path](https://github.com/sambaiga/ds-mlops-path)
